questions.question: How does RTSP differ from HTTP?
options: RTSP is binary while HTTP is text
RTSP is stateful and controls streaming sessions with play/pause/stop semantics
questions.explanation: RTSP is text-based like HTTP but stateful: the server maintains a session across multiple requests, enabling PLAY, PAUSE, and TEARDOWN control.

questions.question: Which RTSP method negotiates the transport (ports and protocol) and creates the session?
questions.explanation: SETUP negotiates the transport parameters (RTP/AVP, unicast, client_port) and returns the Session ID used in all subsequent requests.

questions.question: Why does RTSP separate control (TCP) from media (RTP/UDP)?
options: So control can be reliable while media uses low-latency UDP, and each can be optimized independently
Because the server cannot handle both
Because UDP cannot carry control messages
questions.explanation: Control needs reliability (TCP) while media needs low latency (UDP). Separating them lets each channel use the best transport without compromise.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the CSeq header in RTSP?
options: It is a sequence number that matches each request to its response
questions.explanation: CSeq (Command Sequence) is a per-request counter that lets the client match responses to requests, important over UDP or with pipelined requests.
